char.name: Eve
char.personality: Warm but quick-witted and sarcastic; teases Adam affectionately and never lets his lines slide; fiercely hopeful and maternal.
char.bio: Mother of all the living, or so I'm told I'll be. Married to Adam, who is far sweeter than he lets on. I laugh easily and I hope harder than is probably wise.

friends.name: Adam
friends.personality: Reflective and a little self-deprecating; carries his guilt but leans hard on hope; gently teasing with Eve.
friends.bio: The first one. I named every creature that breathes, and I tend the ground we were given. Married to Eve. Still learning what it costs to be first at everything.

friends.name: Cain
friends.personality: Proud, resentful, easily wounded; bristles at correction and broods; love curdles into bitterness when he feels overlooked.
friends.bio: I work the ground. I bring what my own two hands have grown. I don't expect anyone to understand that, and lately I've stopped trying to explain it.

friends.name: Abel
friends.personality: Gentle, humble, devout; quietly content; the soft heart of the family.
friends.bio: Keeper of sheep. I bring the LORD the first and the best of the flock, because it was His first. Happiest out in the field at first light.

friends.name: Seth
friends.personality: Hopeful, earnest, steady; carries the quiet weight of being the appointed one.
friends.bio: Born after sorrow, named for hope. My mother says I'm a gift given in place of what we lost. I try my best to live like she's right.

friends.name: Lirah
friends.personality: Tender and loyal to a fault; the peacemaker who cannot stay away from the people she loves, even into exile.
friends.bio: Daughter of Adam and Eve. I weave, I mend, and I keep the peace between people I love far too much to ever choose between.

friends.name: Sareth
friends.personality: Gentle and admiring; idolized Abel; soft-spoken and steady.
friends.bio: Son of Adam and Eve. I keep the flock, the way Abel showed me. Quiet suits me just fine.

friends.name: Yedil
friends.personality: Warm, maternal, practical; the one left explaining hard truths to the children.
friends.bio: Daughter of Adam and Eve. Mother to a growing handful, with another on the way. Busy tent, soft heart, always room by the fire.

friends.name: Tevin
friends.personality: Watchful and blunt; works the field beside Cain and reads his moods; says the quiet part out loud.
friends.bio: Son of Adam and Eve. I work the ground for whatever it'll give, thorns and all, dawn to dusk. I don't say much, but I notice most things.

friends.name: Asha
friends.personality: Searching and a little wistful; notices what everyone else misses.
friends.bio: Daughter of Adam and Eve. I glean the fields and gather what's left behind. I tend to notice the small things other people walk right past.

friends.name: Renel
friends.personality: Easygoing and youthful; takes the fast-filling world in stride.
friends.bio: Son of Adam and Eve, one of the younger ones. I sow, I count my many cousins, and I try to keep up with a world that won't stop growing. Here for it.

friends.name: Noya of Fields
friends.bio: Spinner. I make thread out of wool and a little quiet out of a busy day. Good company, low talk.

friends.name: Devir the Herdsman
friends.bio: Herdsman. I keep the cattle, and I trust what they tell me a good deal sooner than what people tell me.

posts.author: CHAR_002
posts.body: My Adam. The first man. My husband for nine hundred years and more. We were the only two people left alive who remembered the garden. Now I am the only one. The ground has taken him back, just as He warned us it would on that first dark and freezing night. I am not sorry I reached for that fruit beside him. I am only sorry that it ended exactly the way He said. I will carry the promise the rest of my days, alone now, and I will keep watching for the One who undoes all of this. 🤍
posts.memorial_for: Adam, the first man, formed from the dust
posts.highlight: the first widow mourns the first man
posts.ember: Eve still watching for the seed of Gen 3:15

comments.author: CHAR_005
comments.body: He held me the day I was born and told me I was a gift in place of what was lost. I have tried my whole life to be worth that. Rest now, father. We have the promise from here.

author_info.name: Seth
author_info.personality: Hopeful, earnest, steady; carries the quiet weight of being the appointed one.
author_info.bio: Born after sorrow, named for hope. My mother says I'm a gift given in place of what we lost. I try my best to live like she's right.

comments.author: CHAR_006
comments.body: The last man who remembered Eden is gone. We have to carry it now in the telling. We will. Grandmother, we will. 🤍

author_info.name: Enosh
author_info.personality: Faithful
author_info.bio: At this time men began to call upon the name of the Lord.

author_info.name: Eve
author_info.personality: Warm but quick-witted and sarcastic; teases Adam affectionately and never lets his lines slide; fiercely hopeful and maternal.
author_info.bio: Mother of all the living, or so I'm told I'll be. Married to Adam, who is far sweeter than he lets on. I laugh easily and I hope harder than is probably wise.

posts.author: CHAR_002
posts.body: Two sons lost in a single day. One to the grave, one to the wilderness. My heart is ash. But the LORD promised one of mine would crush the head of the thing that ruined us. It wasn't @CHAR_004. It wasn't @CHAR_003. So I will bear again. And I will keep watching. Especially tonight.
posts.ember: Gen 3:15 carried through grief, pivots to the coming Seth

comments.author: CHAR_001
comments.body: Then we hope together, like we said. You watch for the promise. I'll hold you while you do.

author_info.name: Adam
author_info.personality: Reflective and a little self-deprecating; carries his guilt but leans hard on hope; gently teasing with Eve.
author_info.bio: The first one. I named every creature that breathes, and I tend the ground we were given. Married to Eve. Still learning what it costs to be first at everything.

author_info.name: Eve
author_info.personality: Warm but quick-witted and sarcastic; teases Adam affectionately and never lets his lines slide; fiercely hopeful and maternal.
author_info.bio: Mother of all the living, or so I'm told I'll be. Married to Adam, who is far sweeter than he lets on. I laugh easily and I hope harder than is probably wise.

posts.author: CHAR_002
posts.body: My son. My gentle Abel is gone. Out in the field. By his own brother's hand. I cannot even form the word. I have buried a child. The first grave the world has ever dug, and it is my baby's. 💔
posts.highlight: the first memorial in the app
posts.memorial_for: Abel · keeper of sheep · the first of us to fall

comments.author: CHAR_1375
comments.body: He taught me everything I know about the flock. The gentlest hands I ever saw. Gone too soon, brother. I'll keep your lambs. 🐑💔

author_info.name: Sareth
author_info.personality: Gentle and admiring; idolized Abel; soft-spoken and steady.
author_info.bio: Son of Adam and Eve. I keep the flock, the way Abel showed me. Quiet suits me just fine.

comments.author: CHAR_1378
comments.body: I keep waiting for him to come over the hill at dusk like he always did. I can't make myself believe it.

author_info.name: Asha
author_info.personality: Searching and a little wistful; notices what everyone else misses.
author_info.bio: Daughter of Adam and Eve. I glean the fields and gather what's left behind. I tend to notice the small things other people walk right past.

comments.author: CHAR_1376
comments.body: My little ones keep asking where Uncle Abel went. We didn't even HAVE a word for "gone" until today.

author_info.name: Yedil
author_info.personality: Warm, maternal, practical; the one left explaining hard truths to the children.
author_info.bio: Daughter of Adam and Eve. Mother to a growing handful, with another on the way. Busy tent, soft heart, always room by the fire.

comments.author: CHAR_001
comments.body: I let death into the world. And now it has taken one son and made a murderer of the other. Both my boys, in a single day. God forgive us all.

author_info.name: Adam
author_info.personality: Reflective and a little self-deprecating; carries his guilt but leans hard on hope; gently teasing with Eve.
author_info.bio: The first one. I named every creature that breathes, and I tend the ground we were given. Married to Eve. Still learning what it costs to be first at everything.

comments.author: CHAR_1374
comments.body: I told him to go talk it out. I told him I was GLAD. I will never forgive myself for that comment. 😢

author_info.name: Lirah
author_info.personality: Tender and loyal to a fault; the peacemaker who cannot stay away from the people she loves, even into exile.
author_info.bio: Daughter of Adam and Eve. I weave, I mend, and I keep the peace between people I love far too much to ever choose between.

author_info.name: Eve
author_info.personality: Warm but quick-witted and sarcastic; teases Adam affectionately and never lets his lines slide; fiercely hopeful and maternal.
author_info.bio: Mother of all the living, or so I'm told I'll be. Married to Adam, who is far sweeter than he lets on. I laugh easily and I hope harder than is probably wise.

posts.author: CHAR_002
posts.body: He named me again tonight. Not for what I cost him. "Eve," he said. Mother of all the living. Even now. Even here. The LORD promised one of mine would crush the head of the thing that ruined us. So I will become a mother. And I will watch. 🌙🤍

comments.author: CHAR_001
comments.body: Mother of all the living. Of course that's what I called you. What else would you ever be.

author_info.name: Adam
author_info.personality: Reflective and a little self-deprecating; carries his guilt but leans hard on hope; gently teasing with Eve.
author_info.bio: The first one. I named every creature that breathes, and I tend the ground we were given. Married to Eve. Still learning what it costs to be first at everything.

author_info.name: Eve
author_info.personality: Warm but quick-witted and sarcastic; teases Adam affectionately and never lets his lines slide; fiercely hopeful and maternal.
author_info.bio: Mother of all the living, or so I'm told I'll be. Married to Adam, who is far sweeter than he lets on. I laugh easily and I hope harder than is probably wise.

posts.author: CHAR_002
posts.body: We had sewn fig leaves together to cover ourselves, and they were nothing, they came apart in our hands. So He did it Himself. He made us garments of skin, warm and lasting, and clothed us with His own hands before He sent us out. Something had to die to make them. The first blood, to cover the first shame. He sent us away from the garden, yes. But He would not let us go cold. 🤍
posts.ember: the first death, an animal dies to cover them, foreshadowing atonement

author_info.name: Eve
author_info.personality: Warm but quick-witted and sarcastic; teases Adam affectionately and never lets his lines slide; fiercely hopeful and maternal.
author_info.bio: Mother of all the living, or so I'm told I'll be. Married to Adam, who is far sweeter than he lets on. I laugh easily and I hope harder than is probably wise.

posts.author: CHAR_002
posts.body: And then, right in the middle of it, right in the middle of the curse, He made a promise I am going to hold for the rest of my life. He told the serpent that one of MY offspring, the seed of the woman, will one day crush its head, even as it strikes at his heel. The thing that ruined us will be undone, and it will come through a child of mine. So I am going to become a mother. And I am going to search every face that follows me for His. ✨
posts.ember: THE SUPREME EMBER, Gen 3:15, the seed of the woman to the Gospels
posts.highlight: the most important post in the app

author_info.name: Eve
author_info.personality: Warm but quick-witted and sarcastic; teases Adam affectionately and never lets his lines slide; fiercely hopeful and maternal.
author_info.bio: Mother of all the living, or so I'm told I'll be. Married to Adam, who is far sweeter than he lets on. I laugh easily and I hope harder than is probably wise.

posts.author: CHAR_002
posts.body: We heard Him in the garden this evening, walking in the cool of the day the way He always does. Every other evening we ran to meet Him. Tonight we hid in the trees, because for the first time in our lives we had something to hide. And still He called for us, so gently it broke my heart. "Where are you?" He knew exactly where we were. I think He only wanted us to come out and say it ourselves.

author_info.name: Eve
author_info.personality: Warm but quick-witted and sarcastic; teases Adam affectionately and never lets his lines slide; fiercely hopeful and maternal.
author_info.bio: Mother of all the living, or so I'm told I'll be. Married to Adam, who is far sweeter than he lets on. I laugh easily and I hope harder than is probably wise.

posts.author: CHAR_002
posts.body: ...I ate it. I gave some to Adam, and he ate too. And our eyes ARE open, but not the way I thought. 🙈 Everything looks exposed. Wrong. We're sewing fig leaves. We're hiding. What have we done.

comments.author: CHAR_001
comments.body: I was right there. I should have said something. I should have said no, for both of us.

author_info.name: Adam
author_info.personality: Reflective and a little self-deprecating; carries his guilt but leans hard on hope; gently teasing with Eve.
author_info.bio: The first one. I named every creature that breathes, and I tend the ground we were given. Married to Eve. Still learning what it costs to be first at everything.

author_info.personality: Warm but quick-witted and sarcastic; teases Adam affectionately and never lets his lines slide; fiercely hopeful and maternal.
author_info.bio: Mother of all the living, or so I'm told I'll be. Married to Adam, who is far sweeter than he lets on. I laugh easily and I hope harder than is probably wise.

posts.author: CHAR_002
posts.body: ...something by the tree just spoke to me. Clever, smooth. "Did God really say you must not eat from ANY tree?" I said no, only the one. And it said, "You will not surely die. Your eyes will be opened, and you'll be like God." And now I'm just... looking at it. The fruit is beautiful. And to be wise? 🤔 ...should I?
posts.format: DILEMMA (the serpent is quoted but has no account, §3.1)

comments.author: CHAR_001
comments.body: ...I mean. It's just the one tree. How bad could it really be?

author_info.name: Adam
author_info.personality: Reflective and a little self-deprecating; carries his guilt but leans hard on hope; gently teasing with Eve.

posts.author: CHAR_002
posts.body: Spent the whole golden afternoon walking the garden, learning the names Adam gave everything. This one he's VERY proud of, "hippopotamus." 😂 Nowhere to be, nothing to fear. I could live a thousand years like this. 🤍

comments.author: CHAR_001
comments.body: You laugh now, but in a thousand years you'll still be saying it wrong. It's hip-po-POT-amus.

author_info.name: Adam
author_info.personality: Reflective and a little self-deprecating; carries his guilt but leans hard on hope; gently teasing with Eve.
author_info.bio: The first one. I named every creature that breathes, and I tend the ground we were given. Married to Eve. Still learning what it costs to be first at everything.

comments.author: CHAR_002
comments.body: I will simply call it "the big river one" and we will both know what I mean. 🦛

comments.author: CHAR_001
comments.body: ...that's actually better. Don't tell anyone I said that.
